pokemon:lanturn.name: Lanturn
pokemon:lanturn.summary: Lanturn is known to emit light. If you peer down into the dark sea from a ship at night, you can sometimes see this Pokémon’s light rising from the depths where it swims. It gives the sea an appearance of a starlit night.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Lanturn is known to emit light. If you peer down into the dark sea from a ship at night, you can sometimes see this Pokémon’s light rising from the depths where it swims. It gives the sea an appearance of a starlit night.
Lanturn’s light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed “The Deep-Sea Star.”
Lanturn’s light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed “The Deep-Sea Star.”
This POKéMON uses the bright part of its body, which changed from a dorsal fin, to lure prey.
LANTURN’s light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed “The Deep-Sea Star.”
The light-emitting orbs on its back are very bright. They are formed from a part of its dorsal fin. This POKéMON illuminates the inky darkness of deep seas.
It blinds prey with an intense burst of light, then swallows the immobilized prey in a single gulp.
The light it emits is so bright that it can illuminate the sea's surface from a depth of over three miles.
The light it emits is so bright that it can illuminate the sea’s surface from a depth of over three miles.
The light it emits is so bright that it can illuminate the sea’s surface from a depth of over three miles.
This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.
Lanturn is nicknamed “the deep-sea star” for its illuminated antenna. This Pokémon produces light by causing a chemical reaction between bacteria and its bodily fluids inside the antenna.
LANTURN’s light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed “The Deep-Sea Star.”
LANTURN’s light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed “The Deep-Sea Star.”
LANTURN is nicknamed “the deep-sea star” for its illuminated antenna. This POKéMON produces light by causing a chemical reaction between bacteria and its bodily fluids inside the antenna.

LANTURN is known to emit light. If you peer down into the dark sea from a ship at night, you can sometimes see this POKéMON’s light rising from the depths where it swims. It gives the sea an appearance of a starlit night.
This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.
It blinds prey with an intense burst of light, then swallows the immobilized prey in a single gulp.
It blinds prey with an intense burst of light. With the prey incapacitated, the Pokémon swallows it in a single gulp.
Should you peer into the ocean at night and see a light shining like the stars, that is Lanturn.
The light it emits is so bright that it can illuminate the sea’s surface from a depth of over three miles.
It lives far down in the depths of the ocean. It blinds its prey with light, using the moment they’re dazzled to swallow them whole.
When the bacteria living inside its antennae absorb Lanturn’s bodily fluids, a strong luminescent effect is produced.
Lanturn’s light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed “The Deep-Sea Star.”
Lanturn’s light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed “The Deep-Sea Star.”
Lanturn’s light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed “The Deep-Sea Star.”
It blinds prey with an intense burst of light. With the prey incapacitated, the Pokémon swallows it in a single gulp.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Lanturn is known to emit light. If you peer down into the dark sea from a ship at night, you can sometimes see this Pokémon’s light rising from the depths where it swims. It gives the sea an appearance of a starlit night.

pokemon:chinchou.name: Chinchou
pokemon:chinchou.summary: Chinchou’s two antennas are filled with cells that generate strong electricity. This Pokémon’s cells create so much electrical power, it even makes itself tingle slightly.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Chinchou’s two antennas are filled with cells that generate strong electricity. This Pokémon’s cells create so much electrical power, it even makes itself tingle slightly.
It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.
It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.
Its antennae, whi­ ch evolved from a fin, have both po­ sitive and neg­ ative charges flo­ wing through them.
It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.
When it senses danger, it discharges positive and negative electricity from its two antennae. It lives in depths beyond sunlight’s reach.
On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.
It shoots positive and negative elec­ tricity between the tips of its two antennae and zaps its enemies.
It shoots positive and negative electricity between the tips of its two antennae and zaps its enemies.
It shoots positive and negative electricity between the tips of its two antennae and zaps its enemies.
It lives in the depths beyond the reach of sunlight. It flashes lights on its antennae to communicate with others of its kind.
Chinchou lets loose positive and negative electrical charges from its two antennas to make its prey faint. This Pokémon flashes its electric lights to exchange signals with others.
It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.
It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.
CHINCHOU lets loose positive and negative electrical charges from its two antennas to make its prey faint. This POKéMON flashes its electric lights to exchange signals with others.
CHINCHOU’s two antennas are filled with cells that generate strong electricity. This POKéMON’s cells create so much electrical power, it even makes itself tingle slightly.
On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.
On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communi­ cation is its constantly flash­ ing lights.
On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.
Its two antennae were originally fins. It discharges electricity to stun its prey before attacking.
Its antennae, which evolved from a fin, have both positive and negative charges flowing through them.
Chinchou blink their shining antennae at one another to claim their respective turf.
Its two antennae glow softly to lure in prey, making it a useful Pokémon for night fishing.
It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.
It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.
On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.
It shoots positive and negative electricity between the tips of its two antennae and zaps its enemies.
